statement.p: Find the y-intercept and any x-intercept(s) of the quadratic curve.

solution.p: To find a parabolas y-intercept, we substitute x=0 into {y}={x^{2}+8x+10}, and we have y=10.
To find a parabolas x-intercept, we substitute y=0 into {y}={x^{2}+8x+10}, and then solve for x:
The polynomial cannot be factored, so we use the Quadratic Formula to solve this equation.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] x \amp = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\ x \amp = \frac{-(8) \pm \sqrt{(8)^{2}-4(1)(10)}}{2(1)} \\ x \amp = \frac{-8 \pm \sqrt{24}}{2} \\ x \amp \approx -1.55051025721682 \text{ or } x \approx -6.44948974278318 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The parabola has two x-intercepts: (-1.55051025721682,0) and (-6.44948974278318,0).
